---
title: "Customer Segmentation Analysis"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
    output-dir: docs
execute:
  echo: false
---



::: {.callout-important}

Key Insight

Customer revenue is highly concentrated, with a small number of enterprise and “whale” customers driving disproportionate value.

The system exhibits a strong Pareto (80/20) structure, with extreme non-linearity in customer value across clusters.
:::



📊 3. Revenue Distribution Across Clusters

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Cluster": ["0","1","2","3","4"],
    "Revenue": [1.2e6, 1.2e6, 9.3e6, 2.5e6, 1.6e6],
})

fig = px.bar(
    df,
    x="Cluster",
    y="Revenue",
    text=df["Revenue"].map(lambda x: f"${x/1e6:.1f}M"),
    title="Total Revenue by Customer Cluster"
)

fig.update_traces(textposition="outside")
fig

df2 = pd.DataFrame({
    "Cluster": ["0","1","2","3","4"],
    "Customers": [960, 940, 2320, 35, 2]
})

fig = px.bar(
    df2,
    x="Cluster",
    y="Customers",
    text="Customers",
    title="Customer Count by Cluster"
)

fig.update_traces(textposition="outside")
fig
